meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Scale Your Freelance Workflow (Intermediate Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with basic apps. Master Git with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship complex features faster and impress your clients today.
content.answer_block: Break through development plateaus by integrating Git-aware AI agents into your terminal. By pairing Antigravity’s architectural scaffolding with Claude Code’s iterative execution, you transform your Git history into a strategic roadmap, allowing you to refactor legacy code, manage complex state, and ship production-ready features with unmatched speed and precision.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: you’re staring at a codebase that has outgrown your initial architecture. What started as a simple MVP is now a tangled web of technical debt, and your client is asking for a feature that feels impossible to implement without breaking the foundation. You aren't just stuck; you're hitting the ceiling of manual development.

It’s time to move beyond standard coding. By weaving AI directly into your Git workflow, you stop 'writing' code and start 'architecting' solutions. This isn't about letting AI take the wheel—it's about deploying a high-speed co-pilot that manages your version control, refactors on the fly, and ensures every commit is a step toward a cleaner, more scalable production environment.

steps.title: Initialize Context-Aware Versioning
steps.description: Establish a robust Git baseline that allows AI agents to understand your project structure, dependencies, and previous architectural decisions before modifying files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and generate a comprehensive .context file that outlines the core business logic, API schema, and critical technical debt markers to guide future AI interventions.

steps.title: Automated Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the structure of your new feature, ensuring it adheres to existing design patterns before writing a single line of implementation code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a scalable folder structure and module interface for a new user authentication flow that integrates with our current database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --plan 'implement auth module based on antigravity schema'

steps.title: Iterative Refactoring with Git Snapshots
steps.description: Perform high-risk refactoring by creating feature branches that allow Claude Code to work in isolated environments, ensuring no regression happens to the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor of the existing state management service to improve performance, ensuring all changes are backward compatible with current API endpoints.

steps.title: AI-Driven Commit Message Synthesis
steps.description: Automate the documentation of your progress. Use AI to generate semantic commit messages that explain the 'why' behind the code, not just the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the changes made in the last three commits into a professional engineering log, highlighting technical improvements and performance gains.

steps.title: Automated Code Review and PR Readiness
steps.description: Before merging, use Claude Code to perform a self-review, checking for edge cases, security vulnerabilities, and adherence to your project's coding standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current branch for potential security vulnerabilities and edge case failures in the new auth logic. Provide a checklist for production readiness.

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'AI-agent' branch to prevent polluting your main production history with experimental AI-generated logic.
Inject your style guide into Antigravity’s context to ensure the AI writes code that mimics your personal coding signature.
Use Claude Code’s 'diff' mode for every major refactor to manually approve logic changes before they are committed to Git.
Treat your '.context' file as a living document; update it after every major feature release to keep the AI aligned with your current architecture.
Leverage Git hooks to automatically trigger AI-based linting whenever you push to a shared development branch.
resources: Git Best Practices for AI Agents: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully integrated these steps, start building a custom 'Agentic Workflow' template that you can clone for every new client project to reduce setup time by 80%.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical debt dictate your growth. Master the tools that turn complex architecture into your competitive advantage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
